title: Geologic controls on up-dip and along-strike propagation of slip during subduction zone earthquakes from a high-resolution seismic reflection survey across the northern limit of slip during the 2010 Mw 8.8 Maule earthquake, offshore Chile
abstract: A grid of closely spaced, high-resolution multichannel seismic (MCS) reflection profiles (1500 km total) was acquired in May 2012 over the outer accretionary prism offshore central Chile (~34°–36°S) using a 48-channel hydrophone streamer. Structure of the deformation front changes abruptly from subduction of most incoming sediment south of the northern limit of Maule slip to nearly complete accretion immediately to the north, occurring over a distance of less than 10 km.

location.description: Outer accretionary prism offshore central Chile ~34°–36°S; northern limit of 2010 Maule earthquake slip zone

seismic_lines.name: ChilePEPPER MCS Grid (CP01–CP25) — Maule northern limit 34°–36°S, 1500 km total, ~2 km line spacing; R/V Atlantis AT26-09, May 2012

data.name: 2012 High-Resolution MCS Grid — Maule Earthquake Northern Limit 34°–36°S
data.description: 1500 km of closely spaced high-resolution MCS reflection profiles (48-channel, May 2012) over the outer accretionary prism at the northern Maule earthquake limit; documents abrupt transition from sediment subduction to accretion over <10 km. [R/V Atlantis AT26-09 cruise data available via RVData (Rolling Deck to Repository)]

data.name: Swath Bathymetry — Maule Northern Limit Survey 2012
data.description: Multibeam bathymetry from 2012 survey offshore central Chile in the northern Maule earthquake zone

data.name: Sidescan
data.description: acquired during R/V Atlantis expedition AT26-09 (2013)

_data_note: AT26-09 cruise data (R/V Atlantis, ChilePEPPER, May 2012) accessible via RVData at rvdata.us/search/cruise/AT26-09.

processing.summary: 1500 km of closely spaced high-resolution MCS reflection profiles (48-channel hydrophone streamer) were acquired in May 2012 over the outer accretionary prism at the northern limit of Maule earthquake slip (~34°–36°S). Standard processing through NMO, stack and post-stack migration yields TWT sections. The survey reveals an abrupt transition over <10 km between sediment subduction to the south and near-complete accretion to the north.
processing.notes: High-resolution 48-channel data; closely spaced grid designed to image deformation front morphology

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. CRITICAL: authors completely wrong (was 20 ChilePEPPER project authors) → 7 actual paper authors: Tréhu A.M.(OSU), Hass B., de Moor A., Maksymowicz A. (U. Chile), Contreras-Reyes E. (U. Chile), Vera E. (U. Chile), Tryon M.D. (Scripps). vessel: R/V Melville → R/V Atlantis (AT26-09, ChilePEPPER, May 2012). seismic_lines: named CP01–CP25 (ChilePEPPER lines). Paper also references MV1610 R/V Roger Revelle data and R/V Vidal Gormaz VG02-03 data for comparison. Data at rvdata.us/search/cruise/AT26-09 confirmed open.
